steps.title: Identify the y-intercept condition
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis. On the y-axis, the x-coordinate is always 0. Therefore, to find the y-intercept, we substitute $x = 0$ into the equation.

steps.title: Substitute x = 0 and simplify
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 0$ into $y = 8x^2 + 59$:
$$y = 8(0)^2 + 59$$
$$y = 0 + 59$$
$$y = 59$$
Therefore, the value of $y$ at the y-intercept is 59.
